ops.text: `uv run --with <local-directory>` serves a STALE cached wheel keyed on (name, version): after editing the package's source at an unchanged version, uv kept serving the previously built wheel through multiple `--reinstall`/`--refresh` attempts. Cost during the gd-build per-item-static-fallback work: one false verification pass ran only 10 of 11 patches because the just-added patch wasn't in the served wheel. Workaround that worked: copy the tree to a throwaway dir and bump the version (e.g. 0.1.0 → 0.1.99) so the cache key changes. Non-issue for CI's `--with git+...@main` form (git-SHA keyed). Any local gd-build (or other path-dep) testing instruction should mandate the version-bumped throwaway copy, not trust --reinstall.
